name: Bruce Marder's Flourless Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
2. Generously brush a 9-inch round cake pan with melted butter and sprinkle with sugar.
3. Line the bottom of the pan with parchment paper or wax paper.
4. In a food processor fitted with a knife blade, cream the butter until smooth.
5. Add almonds and beat until the mixture comes together.
6. Add melted, cooled chocolate and blend well.
7. Add egg yolks one at a time and continue blending until smooth and fluffy.
8. In a large bowl of an electric mixer, beat egg whites until foamy.
9. Gradually add sugar to the egg whites, beating until smooth and firm.
10. Fold in 1/3 of the egg white mixture into the batter to lighten it.
11. Gently fold in the remaining egg whites.
12. Spoon the batter into the prepared cake pan.
13. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 10 minutes, then reduce the temperature to 250°F (120°C).
14. Continue baking for 1 hour and 30 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out dry.
15. Cool on a rack.
16. Invert the cake onto a serving plate and peel off the parchment paper.
17. To make the chocolate glaze, combine butter, honey, and water in a heavy saucepan.
18. Bring to a boil, remove from heat, and add chopped chocolate.
19. Beat until stiff enough to spread or drizzle over the cake.
20. If the glaze is not thick enough, return it to low heat and simmer for 3-5 minutes, mixing constantly.
